Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The 'Results' section on page 1 states: "Between 05 October 2005 and 31 March 2013, 1086 M1 patients were randomised to receive SOC (n = 724) or SOC + docetaxel (n = 362)." The 'Methods' section on page 2 also states: "We randomly allocated patients in 2:1 ratio to standard-of-care (SOC; control group) or SOC + docetaxel." This indicates 'SOC + docetaxel' is the experimental arm.

Control Arm.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'We randomly allocated patients in 2:1 ratio to standard-of-care (SOC; control group) or SOC + docetaxel.' The 'Introduction' section on page 2 further clarifies: 'men with newly diagnosed metastatic prostate cancer were randomised on a 2:1 basis either to a standard-of-care (SOC) control group ('Control') or SOC + docetaxel treatment group ('docetaxel') [1].' This identifies 'standard-of-care (SOC; control group)' as the control arm.

Treatment Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Results' section, it states: 'Between 05 October 2005 and 31 March 2013, 1086 M1 patients were randomised to receive SOC (n = 724) or SOC + docetaxel (n = 362).' The 'SOC + docetaxel' arm is the experimental arm, with 362 participants. This is confirmed by Figure 1 (Consort diagram) on page 4, which shows '362 M1 docetaxel arm' under the 'Research' allocation and '362 analysed for long-term efficacy'.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: On page 3, under the 'Results' section, it states: 'The median duration of follow-up was 78.2 months (inter-quartile range (IQR): 62.9–96.3).'

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: Chemotherapy (docetaxel) and hormonal therapy (androgen deprivation therapy)
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states: 'We randomly allocated patients in 2:1 ratio to standard-of-care (SOC; control group) or SOC + docetaxel.' The 'Introduction' section on page 2 further clarifies: 'SOC in M1 patients comprised long-term androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), the intervention being lifelong ADT and six cycles of docetaxel at standard dose.' Therefore, Treatment Arm 1 consists of docetaxel (a chemotherapy agent) and androgen deprivation therapy (a hormonal therapy agent).
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: lifelong ADT and six cycles of docetaxel (75 mg/m²) given 3-weekly with prednisolone 5 mg twice daily during the 18-week period of therapy
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: On page 2, under the 'Introduction' section, it states: 'men with newly diagnosed metastatic prostate cancer were randomised on a 2:1 basis either to a standard-of-care (SOC) control group ('Control') or SOC + docetaxel treatment group ('docetaxel') [1]. SOC in M1 patients comprised long-term androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), the intervention being lifelong ADT and six cycles of docetaxel at standard dose.' Further details are provided under 'Procedures' on page 2: 'In brief, patients were randomised to lifelong ADT with or without six cycles of docetaxel (75 mg/m²) given 3-weekly with prednisolone 5 mg twice daily during the 18-week period of therapy.' This describes the experimental treatment arm.

Total Participants - N.evidence: On page 1, under the 'Results' section, it states: 'Between 05 October 2005 and 31 March 2013, 1086 M1 patients were randomised to receive SOC (n = 724) or SOC + docetaxel (n = 362).' This indicates a total of 1086 participants.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The 'Methods' section on page 1 states, "We randomly allocated patients in 2:1 ratio to standard-of-care (SOC; control group) or SOC + docetaxel." This indicates two arms: a control group and a docetaxel treatment group. The 'Results' section on page 3 further confirms this by stating, "One thousand and eighty-six metastatic patients (724 control/362 docetaxel) were recruited to STAMPEDE's 'docetaxel comparison'." Figure 1 on page 4 also visually represents these two arms: '1184 allocated standard-of-care (control)' and '592 allocated docetaxel (research) arm'.

Control Arm - N.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Results' section, it states: 'Between 05 October 2005 and 31 March 2013, 1086 M1 patients were randomised to receive SOC (n = 724) or SOC + docetaxel (n = 362).' SOC refers to the standard-of-care, which is the control group. This is confirmed in Figure 1, 'Consort diagram', under 'Allocation', where '1184 allocated standard-of-care (control)' is broken down to '724 M1 standard-of-care'.

Trial Name.evidence: Found in the title of the article: "Addition of docetaxel to hormonal therapy in low- and high-burden metastatic hormone sensitive prostate cancer: long-term survival results from the STAMPEDE trial".

Author.evidence: Found in the author list at the beginning of the article: "N. W. Clarke, A. Ali, F. C. Ingleby, A. Hoyle, C. L. Amos, G. Attard, C. D. Brawley, J. Calvert, S. Chowdhury, A. Cook, W. Cross, D. P. Dearnaley, H. Douis, D. Gilbert, S. Gillessen, R. J. Jones, R. E. Langley, A. MacNair, Z. Malik, M. D. Mason, D. Matheson, R. Millman, C. C. Parker, A. W. S. Ritchie, H. Rush, J. M. Russell, J. Brown, S. Beesley, A. Birtle, L. Capaldi, J. Gale, S. Gibbs, A. Lydon, A. Nikapota, A. Omlin, J. M. O'Sullivan, O. Parikh, A. Protheroe, S. Rudman, N. N. Srihari, M. Simms, J. S. Tanguay, S. Tolan, J. Wagstaff, J. Wallace, J. Wylie, A. Zarkar, M. R. Sydes, M. K. B. Parmar, N. D. James & the STAMPEDE investigators".

Year.evidence: Found in the publication details at the top right of page 1: "Annals of Oncology 30: 1992-2003, 2019".

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is labeled 'ORIGINAL ARTICLE' and contains extensive sections including Introduction, Materials and methods, Results, Discussion, and References, indicating it is a full publication rather than an an abstract.

Phase.evidence: The article refers to the STAMPEDE trial as a 'randomised controlled trial' and compares its results with other 'phase 3' trials like CHAARTED and GETUG-AFU-15. Reference [13] on page 11 explicitly states 'STAMPEDE: a randomised controlled phase 3 trial'.

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The title mentions 'long-term survival results' and the Background section states 'STAMPEDE has previously reported that the use of upfront docetaxel improved overall survival (OS)... We report on long-term outcomes...'. The Introduction further clarifies that this is an update to a primary analysis, with 'long-term follow-up results' being analysed.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The document states that docetaxel was given for an '18-week period of therapy' (4.5 months) as part of the treatment arm, which also included 'lifelong ADT'. However, the median duration for the entire 'on-treatment therapy' for the treatment arm is not explicitly reported. Median survival is reported as 59.1 months, but this is not the same as on-treatment duration.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.evidence: The control arm received 'lifelong ADT'. While the median survival for the control group is reported as 43.1 months, the median 'on-treatment duration' for the lifelong ADT is not explicitly stated in the document.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: On page 4, Figure 1 (Consort diagram) under the 'Safety' section for the 'Research' arm, it states '333 received docetaxel (0 control arm; 333 research arm)'. The text below Figure 1 further clarifies that 'Twenty-nine metastatic patients allocated to Docetaxel never reported starting chemotherapy'. Since 362 patients were allocated to the SOC + docetaxel arm (treatment arm) as per page 1 'Results' and page 4 Figure 1, 333 out of 362 received docetaxel, which is (333/362)*100 = 91.99%, rounded to 92%.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: On page 4, Figure 1 (Consort diagram) under the 'Safety' section, it explicitly states '333 received docetaxel (0 control arm; 333 research arm)', indicating that 0 patients in the control arm received docetaxel. The control arm was 'standard-of-care (SOC)' which is ADT alone, as described in the 'Procedures' section on page 2.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Baseline characteristics, for the subset of 830/1086 patients included in metastatic burden sub-group analysis, by trial arm', on page 6. Under 'Metastatic burden', 'High' row, 'Docetaxel' column.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Baseline characteristics, for the subset of 830/1086 patients included in metastatic burden sub-group analysis, by trial arm', on page 6. Under 'Metastatic burden', 'High' row, 'Control' column.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Found in Table 2, 'Baseline characteristics, for the subset of 830/1086 patients included in metastatic burden sub-group analysis, by trial arm', on page 6. Under 'Metastatic burden', 'Low' row, 'Control' column.

Median Age (years) | Treatment.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline characteristics for all metastatic patients, by trial arm', on page 4, under 'Age at randomisation (years)', 'Median' row, in the 'Docetaxel' column.

Median Age (years) | Control.evidence: Found in Table 1, 'Baseline characteristics for all metastatic patients, by trial arm', on page 4, under 'Age at randomisation (years)', 'Median' row, in the 'Control' column.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study focuses on "newly diagnosed mHNPC patients" (Page 6, Discussion) and "metastatic hormone naïve prostate cancer patients" (Page 1, Background), which refers to synchronous metastases (metastases present at the time of diagnosis).

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The document does not report results based on metachronous disease. It focuses on "newly diagnosed" or "metastatic hormone naïve" prostate cancer, implying synchronous disease.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: The title of the article mentions "high-burden metastatic hormone sensitive prostate cancer". The 'Background' section on Page 1 states, "We report on long-term outcomes stratified by metastatic burden for M1 patients." The 'Procedures' section on Page 2 defines "high-burden patients". The 'Results' section on Page 3 and 'Table 2' on Page 6 present data for "high-burden patients".

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: The title of the article mentions "low- and high-burden metastatic hormone sensitive prostate cancer". The 'Background' section on Page 1 states, "We report on long-term outcomes stratified by metastatic burden for M1 patients." The 'Procedures' section on Page 2 defines "low metastatic burden". The 'Results' section on Page 3 and 'Table 2' on Page 6 present data for "low-burden patients".

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is explicitly mentioned in the title: "long-term survival results from the STAMPEDE trial". It is also referenced multiple times throughout the document, for example, in the 'Background' section: "STAMPEDE has previously reported..." and in the 'Introduction' section: "The primary analysis of STAMPEDE's 'docetaxel comparison'..." and "The multi-arm, multi-stage STAMPEDE trial enrols patients..."

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document focuses on survival outcomes (overall survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, prostate cancer-specific survival) and toxicity. There is no mention of a 'Quality of Life Scale' or any assessment of participants' overall well-being or satisfaction with health status using a standardized questionnaire or survey.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: The document discusses various outcomes such as overall survival, failure-free survival, progression-free survival, metastatic progression-free survival, and prostate cancer-specific survival, as well as toxicity and adverse events. However, there is no mention or reporting of 'Quality of Life' of participants throughout the entire document.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: Found in Table 1, "Baseline characteristics for all metastatic patients, by trial arm", under the "Docetaxel" column, "Metastatic burdenª" row. The sum of "Low" (124, 34%) and "High" (148, 41%) burden patients, who are defined as metastatic at baseline (synchronous), is 272 (75%).

Found in Table 1, "Baseline characteristics for all metastatic patients, by trial arm", under the "Control" column, "Metastatic burdenª" row. The sum of "Low" (238, 33%) and "High" (320, 44%) burden patients, who are defined as metastatic at baseline (synchronous), is 558 (77%).

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The document reports on patients who were metastatic at baseline (synchronous) and categorizes them by 'metastatic burden'. There is no information provided on patients who developed metastases after the initial diagnosis (metachronous metastases) in the treatment arm.

The document reports on patients who were metastatic at baseline (synchronous) and categorizes them by 'metastatic burden'. There is no information provided on patients who developed metastases after the initial diagnosis (metachronous metastases) in the control arm.

Secondary Endpoint(s).value: failure-free survival (FFS; time from randomisation to the first of any: biochemical, lymph node, distant metastatic progression or prostate cancer death); progression-free survival (PFS; time from randomisation to the first FFS event, not including biochemical progression); metastatic progression-free survival (mPFS; time from randomisation to either new metastases, progression of existing metastases or prostate cancer death) and prostate cancer-specific survival (PCSS; time from randomisation to prostate cancer death)
Secondary Endpoint(s).evidence: On page 2, under the 'Outcomes' section, the text explicitly states: 'Secondary outcomes for long-term efficacy data included: failure-free survival (FFS; time from randomisation to the first of any: biochemical, lymph node, distant metastatic progression or prostate cancer death); progression-free survival (PFS; time from randomisation to the first FFS event, not including biochemical progression); metastatic progression-free survival (mPFS; time from randomisation to either new metastases, progression of existing metastases or prostate cancer death) and prostate cancer-specific survival (PCSS; time from randomisation to prostate cancer death).'

Primary Endpoint(s).value: Overall survival was specified as the primary efficacy outcome and defined as time from randomisation to death from any cause.

No. of Deaths - N | Treatment.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Results' section, it states: 'There were 719 deaths reported; 494/724 (68%) control patients died compared with 225/362 (62%) docetaxel patients.' The docetaxel group is the treatment arm.

No. of Deaths - N | Control.evidence: On page 1, in the 'Results' section, it states: 'There were 719 deaths reported; 494/724 (68%) control patients died compared with 225/362 (62%) docetaxel patients.' The control group is the SOC arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Found in Table 4, 'Worst adverse event grade reported per patient (across all CTCAE categories) for (i) up to one year on the trial', under the 'Docetaxel' column. The sum of Grade 3 (96, 29%), Grade 4 (44, 13%), and Grade 5 (1, <1%) adverse events is 141 patients, which represents 42% of the 333 patients analyzed for safety in the docetaxel arm. This is also explicitly stated in the text on page 4: '42% docetaxel reported G3-5 toxicity'.

Found in Table 4, 'Worst adverse event grade reported per patient (across all CTCAE categories) for (i) up to one year on the trial', under the 'Control' column. The sum of Grade 3 (157, 21%), Grade 4 (20, 3%), and Grade 5 (2, <1%) adverse events is 179 patients, which represents 24% of the 753 patients analyzed for safety in the control arm. This is also explicitly stated in the text on page 4: '24% in Control'.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document reports 'all-cause' Grade 3 or higher adverse events in Table 4 and the surrounding text on page 4, but does not provide a breakdown for 'treatment-related' Grade 3 or higher adverse events specifically for the treatment arm.

The document reports 'all-cause' Grade 3 or higher adverse events in Table 4 and the surrounding text on page 4, but does not provide a breakdown for 'treatment-related' Grade 3 or higher adverse events specifically for the control arm.
The document reports 'all-cause' Grade 5 adverse events in Table 4 for the treatment arm (1, <1% up to one year), but does not specify if these were 'treatment-related'.
The document reports 'all-cause' Grade 5 adverse events in Table 4 for the control arm (2, <1% up to one year), but does not specify if these were 'treatment-related'.

Add-on Treatment.evidence: The title of the article is "Addition of docetaxel to hormonal therapy...". The 'Methods' section states: "We randomly allocated patients in 2:1 ratio to standard-of-care (SOC; control group) or SOC + docetaxel." It further clarifies that "SOC in M1 patients comprised long-term androgen deprivation therapy (ADT), the intervention being lifelong ADT and six cycles of docetaxel at standard dose." This indicates docetaxel is the additional therapy given in combination with ADT.
